assumptions.name: use_amount_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product amount per event was supplied in milliliters and converted with density.

assumptions.name: density_g_per_ml

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP heuristic density screening defaults

assumptions.rationale: Default density used for volume-to-mass conversion in screening mode, resolved from product category, physical form, and product subtype when available.

assumptions.name: product_mass_g_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Converted volume-based use amount into mass using density and, when needed, a bounded pressurized-aerosol interpretation factor.

assumptions.name: concentration_fraction
assumptions.rationale: Chemical fraction in the product was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: use_events_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Use frequency was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: chemical_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Product mass per event multiplied by concentration fraction.

assumptions.name: aerosolized_fraction

source.title: Characterization of airborne particles from cleaning sprays and their corresponding respiratory deposition fractions

assumptions.rationale: Aerosolized fraction defaulted from application_method='trigger_spray' and product_category='household_cleaner'.

assumptions.name: room_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Room volume was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: air_exchange_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Air exchange rate defaulted from the shared inhalation defaults pack.

assumptions.name: exposure_duration_hours
assumptions.rationale: Exposure duration defaulted from the shared inhalation defaults pack.

assumptions.name: inhalation_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Inhalation rate was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: body_weight_kg
assumptions.rationale: Body weight was supplied explicitly for dose normalization.

assumptions.name: vapor_pressure_mmhg
assumptions.rationale: Vapor pressure was supplied explicitly for bounded volatility-aware inhalation screening.

assumptions.name: molecular_weight_g_per_mol
assumptions.rationale: Molecular weight was supplied explicitly for bounded volatility-aware inhalation screening.

assumptions.name: log_kow
assumptions.rationale: logKow was supplied explicitly for chemistry-aware screening context.

assumptions.name: water_solubility_mg_per_l
assumptions.rationale: Water solubility was supplied explicitly for chemistry-aware screening context.

assumptions.name: source_distance_m
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 source distance was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: spray_duration_seconds
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 active spray duration was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: near_field_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 near-field volume was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: airflow_directionality
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 airflow directionality class was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: particle_size_regime
assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 particle-size regime was supplied explicitly.

assumptions.name: deposition_rate_per_hour

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP heuristic inhalation deposition-sink defaults

assumptions.rationale: Deposition sink defaulted from the bounded inhalation physical-caps pack.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_reference_temperature_c

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP volatility saturation-cap policy for room-air screening

assumptions.rationale: Volatility saturation cap uses the governed room-temperature screening policy.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_mg_per_m3

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP volatility saturation-cap policy for room-air screening

assumptions.rationale: Thermodynamic saturation ceiling derived from vapor pressure, molecular weight, and the governed room-temperature policy.

assumptions.name: extrathoracic_swallow_fraction

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP bounded extrathoracic oral-handoff heuristics for spray inhalation screening

assumptions.rationale: Extrathoracic swallowed fraction defaulted from the bounded spray physiology handoff pack.

assumptions.name: released_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Released mass = chemical mass per event x aerosolized fraction.

assumptions.name: total_loss_rate_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Total room-air loss rate = air exchange rate + deposition sink.

assumptions.name: spray_duration_hours
assumptions.rationale: Converted Tier 1 spray duration from seconds into hours.

assumptions.name: far_field_volume_m3
assumptions.rationale: Far-field volume = room volume minus near-field volume.

assumptions.name: near_field_exchange_turnover_per_hour

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP Tier 1 NF/FF screening parameter pack

assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 screening turnover resolved from the packaged airflow-directionality profile.

assumptions.name: thermal_plume_rate_m3_per_hour

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP bounded Tier 1 local-entrainment heuristics for NF/FF spray screening

assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 local entrainment floor includes a bounded thermal-plume entrainment term.

assumptions.name: spray_jet_rate_m3_per_hour

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP bounded Tier 1 local-entrainment heuristics for NF/FF spray screening

assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 local entrainment floor includes a bounded spray-jet entrainment term resolved from application method.

assumptions.name: particle_persistence_factor

source.title: Direct-Use Exposure MCP Tier 1 NF/FF screening parameter pack

assumptions.rationale: Tier 1 persistence factor resolved from the packaged particle-regime profile.

assumptions.name: static_interzonal_mixing_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Static interzonal mixing rate = near-field volume x (screening exchange turnover + room ventilation).

assumptions.name: local_entrainment_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Bounded local entrainment floor = thermal-plume entrainment + application-method spray-jet entrainment.

assumptions.name: interzonal_mixing_rate_m3_per_hour
assumptions.rationale: Interzonal mixing rate equals the greater of the static Tier 1 exchange estimate and the bounded local entrainment floor.

assumptions.name: interzonal_mixing_floor_applied
assumptions.rationale: Whether the bounded local entrainment floor exceeded the static Tier 1 interzonal rate.

assumptions.name: far_field_average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Far-field room average derived from the Tier 1 room model over the full exposure window after bounded loss terms are applied.

assumptions.name: uncapped_far_field_average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped far-field room average before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: near_field_active_spray_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Active spray breathing-zone concentration derived from a screening near-field increment added to the far-field room average.

assumptions.name: uncapped_near_field_active_spray_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped active-spray breathing-zone concentration before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: breathing_zone_time_weighted_average_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Time-weighted breathing-zone average over the full event duration.

assumptions.name: uncapped_average_air_concentration_mg_per_m3
assumptions.rationale: Uncapped breathing-zone time-weighted average before any volatility saturation cap is applied.

assumptions.name: room_air_decay_half_life_hours
assumptions.rationale: Room-air decay half-life implied by air exchange plus the bounded deposition sink.

assumptions.name: saturation_cap_applied
assumptions.rationale: Whether the volatility saturation cap constrained one or more modeled Tier 1 room-air concentrations.

assumptions.name: inhaled_mass_mg_per_event
assumptions.rationale: Inhaled mass per event combines active-spray near-field and remaining far-field periods.

assumptions.name: inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Daily inhaled mass equals inhaled mass per event multiplied by events/day.

assumptions.name: swallowed_extrathoracic_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Extrathoracic swallowed mass per day derived from the bounded swallowed fraction applied to the screening inhaled mass.

assumptions.name: lower_respiratory_inhaled_mass_mg_per_day
assumptions.rationale: Lower-respiratory inhaled mass per day after subtracting the bounded extrathoracic swallowed share from the screening inhaled mass.

assumptions.name: normalized_external_dose_mg_per_kg_day
assumptions.rationale: Normalized external dose = inhaled mass per day / body weight.

notes: All defaults are surfaced through exposureAssumptionRecord entries.

limitations.message: Spray inhalation includes a bounded extrathoracic swallowed-fraction estimate. This is not a full regional deposition model or an automatic oral handoff into a separate ingestion scenario.
Tier 1 NF/FF uses a deterministic screening exchange-rate mapping from airflow_directionality rather than measured interzonal airflow.
Particle-size effects use bounded screening persistence and deposition terms and do not model droplet evaporation or full aerosol dynamics.

quality_flags.message: Default value for 'density_g_per_ml' comes from a heuristic screening source rather than a curated factor pack.
Tier 1 inhalation resolves a screening near-field/far-field split, but remains a deterministic external-dose model rather than a calibrated aerosol simulator.

tier_semantics.tier_rationale: Inhalation output uses a deterministic near-field/far-field screening split for spray events with explicit geometry and airflow-class inputs.
required_caveats: Interpret the near-field compartment as a governed screening construct rather than a measured CFD domain.
The airflow directionality and particle regime terms remain screening classifications, not measured aerosol dynamics.
Very weak static interzonal mixing can be bounded upward by a local entrainment floor from thermal-plume and spray-jet heuristics.
Bounded deposition and volatility caps improve physical realism but do not turn the model into a transient aerosol transport solver.
forbidden_interpretations: Do not interpret this result as deposited dose, absorbed dose, or a PBPK state.
Do not treat the screening exchange mapping as a validated transient aerosol dispersion model.
Do not treat the result as a final risk conclusion.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Tier A deterministic screening boundary remains in force
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The current result is governed as tier_a and tier_1; it is auditable but not probabilistic.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use named scenario envelopes for Tier B refinement before attempting probabilistic propagation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Bounded aerosol deposition sink constrains inhalation persistence
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Inhalation persistence is reduced with a bounded first-order deposition sink rather than a full aerosol deposition and evaporation solver.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat deposition-sensitive spray outputs as physically bounded screening estimates and prefer benchmarked particle families.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Coarse-spray extrathoracic swallowing is represented as a bounded handoff
uncertaintyRegister.summary: A portion of spray mass is routed to extrathoracic swallowing with a bounded regime-dependent screening fraction.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Keep inhalation and incidental-oral interpretation linked for coarse sprays and avoid treating the handoff as a deposition dosimetry model.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Resolved assumption `density_g_per_ml` carries default uncertainty
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Replace this default with scenario-specific evidence or include it in a Tier B envelope as an explicit variant driver.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `extrathoracic_oral_handoff_screening` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Spray inhalation includes a bounded extrathoracic swallowed-fraction estimate. This is not a full regional deposition model or an automatic oral handoff into a separate ingestion scenario.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `near_field_exchange_screening` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Tier 1 NF/FF uses a deterministic screening exchange-rate mapping from airflow_directionality rather than measured interzonal airflow.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Limitation `particle_regime_screening` remains unquantified
uncertaintyRegister.summary: Particle-size effects use bounded screening persistence and deposition terms and do not model droplet evaporation or full aerosol dynamics.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Treat this as a model or scenario uncertainty and keep it visible in downstream interpretation.

uncertaintyRegister.title: Known dependencies are preserved qualitatively, not numerically
uncertaintyRegister.summary: The output carries known dependence structures, but no joint sampling or correlation model is applied in Tier A.
uncertaintyRegister.recommendation: Use scenario packaging for Tier B and reserve probabilistic propagation for later tiers with explicit joint models.

dependencyMetadata.title: Use amount and frequency should be treated as a behavioral package
dependencyMetadata.note: Tier A/B keeps use amount and use frequency in named scenario packages instead of sampling them independently.

dependencyMetadata.title: Body size and physiology should scale together
dependencyMetadata.note: Future probabilistic tiers should use a joint physiological model rather than independent sampling.

dependencyMetadata.title: Room size, ventilation, and duration form one scenario context
dependencyMetadata.note: Microenvironment parameters should be changed as coherent room archetypes instead of independent marginals.

dependencyMetadata.title: Aerosol release depends on spray mechanism and physical form
dependencyMetadata.note: Later uncertainty tiers should condition aerosol release on the spray mechanism rather than sample it independently.

dependencyMetadata.title: Tier 1 NF/FF geometry and timing should move together
dependencyMetadata.note: Tier 1 spray geometry, timing, and airflow classes should remain a coherent scenario package rather than independent marginals.

executedValidationChecks.title: Trigger-spray cleaner airborne fraction vs ready-to-use cleaning spray study
executedValidationChecks.note: Observed aerosolized_fraction is compared against the reported 2.7%-32.2% airborne-mass range for ready-to-use trigger cleaning sprays.

executedValidationChecks.title: Trigger-spray aerosol decay half-life vs climate-chamber spray study
executedValidationChecks.note: Observed room-air decay half-life is compared against a screening band centered on the reported 0.25 h average trigger-spray aerosol half-life from the 2023 cleaning and disinfection spray chamber study.

notes: Deterministic verification and benchmark regression are available for this domain.
External validation remains a documented future capability rather than an active gate.
Heuristic assumption names are emitted explicitly so evidence gaps stay attached to the scenario rather than hidden in source packs.
Probabilistic outputs stay disabled until dependencies and validation evidence mature.
Executable validation checks were run because the scenario matched one or more published reference patterns.

interpretation_notes: Deterministic Tier 1 inhalation scenario with a screening near-field/far-field split.
The near-field increment is active during spray duration only and reverts to the far-field room average for the remainder of the event.
Matched packaged Tier 1 screening profile `household_cleaner_trigger_spray_tier1`; caller-supplied Tier 1 geometry and regime inputs remain authoritative.
